topic: Post-integration adversarial review of Section 4.1 full restructure (EXP-182 fold-in: 28-domain audit table, 47.69σ / 12-knob framing, Pearson r = 0.1727 / R² = 0.0298 coexistence wedge, 520-p-values Fisher independence critique, 5-class taxonomy applied-to-9-claims finding)
change_summary: Full restructure of SEC-4.1 from 18,959-char c4 version to 27,895-char rewrite. EXP-182 folded in per commit 191a3d4 (2026-04-18 10:39 local), superseding EXP-178/179/180. New content: (a) 28-row audit table with non-overlapping classifications — 14 STATIC, 7 BORROWED, 2 TAUTOLOGY, 2 THEORY-FRAMEWORK, 2 LOOSE, 1 DISCRIMINATING; (b) 12-knob recount against formula_runtime.json (FORM-V51-001..006 + 3 master constants); (c) new sub-section 'How 47.69σ coexists with R² = 0.0298' with arithmetic Fisher worked example at p=0.1 and p=0.05; (d) new sub-section on '520 historical domain p-values' violating Fisher independence in time (~19 repeats × 28 domains, √19 ≈ 4.4× deflation); (e) divided-audience framing (human live.html vs AI auditor ai_manifest.json / methodology.json); (f) taxonomy-applied-to-9-of-69+ claims critique as falsifiable canary.
our_argument_summary: The section audits the dome April-2026 live.html dashboard expansion to 28 domains and 47.69σ Fisher-combined significance. Core thesis: (1) the same panel shows 47.69σ directly alongside R² = 0.0298 — fit quality measure contradicts the combined-significance headline; (2) Fisher's method assumes independence, but the 520-p-value input set violates independence on two stacked axes — shared fitted constants across domains (λ_g, κ, v_a, r_avg) AND ~19× repeated daily measurements of the same time-stationary checks; (3) the full dome formula pack (formula_runtime.json FORM-V51-001..006 plus three master constants) contains only 12 independent numerical knobs — 28 dashboard labels / 12 knobs = 2.33× label inflation, before the time-repeat inflation stacks on top; (4) non-overlapping classification of the 28 rows produces 14 STATIC, 7 BORROWED, 2 TAUTOLOGY, 2 THEORY-FRAMEWORK, 2 LOOSE, 1 DISCRIMINATING (NMP Longitude) — after removing categories that are not fresh empirical tests, 100%/28-of-28 reduces to ~1 genuinely discriminating cell; (5) the dome author added AI-auditor-facing caveats in ai_manifest.json / methodology.json explicitly labeling the tombstone counts as 'bookkeeping, not validation' and instructing AI readers to separate discriminating from supportive items, but the human-facing live.html panel still leads with 100% / 47.69σ tombstones — acknowledge-without-fix pattern matching the OpenTimestamps canary; (6) the 5-class taxonomy is applied to only 9 of 69+ registered claims in claim_index.json — the rigor improvement is theoretical until broadly applied, and the classified-count growth rate is a falsifiable test.

tldr_review.notes: TLDR is 5 sentences, within bounds. Claims: (a) dome's Live Power panel shows 47.69σ Fisher, '28 of 28 passing at 100.0%', Pearson r = 0.1727, R² = 0.0298 — matches body text and direct live.html citations. (b) 'Simultaneously advertising evidence exceeds single-discovery significance by multiple orders of magnitude and showing predictions explain about 3% of the variance' — the phrase inside the non-quoted paraphrase is actually a direct quote from the dome caption, and 3% = R² of 0.0298 is numerically correct. (c) 'methodology.json defines a five-class claim taxonomy, but as of this writing it has been applied to only 9 of the site's 69+ claims' — matches body claim_index.json count (4+2+1+1+1 = 9). (d) Non-overlap table tally '14 static WIN re-references, 7 borrowed globe values, 2 framework recastings, 2 loose checks, 2 tautologies, 1 discriminator' — sums to 28 correctly (14+7+2+2+2+1=28), matches body non-overlap tally exactly. No TLDR factual errors. Very minor: the inner phrase 'evidence exceeds single-discovery significance by multiple orders of magnitude' IS a verbatim quote from the dome but TLDR presents it as paraphrase — not worth flagging.

relevant_files: dome's ai_manifest.json (2026-04-17 agent_rules and registry_semantics blocks)
dome's api/current/claim_index.json (9 classified claims as of 2026-04-18)
dome's live.html V51.2 Automated Systems Audit panel
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Section audits the dome's live dashboard Fisher-σ aggregation (520 historical domain p-values → -2∑ ln(p_i) = χ²(2k=1040) → σ via standard-normal conversion). Our review explicitly works Fisher arithmetic at p=0.1 (χ²≈2393, ~30σ) and p=0.05 (χ²≈3115, ~45σ), bracketing the 47.69σ the dome displays. No simulation — pure analytic Fisher combination — which is the correct tool here.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Our review's numerical claims are all computed against the dome's own published artifacts (formula_runtime.json, methodology.json, claim_index.json). The 12-knob count, 2.33× ratio, 9-of-69 classified claims count, and Fisher-arithmetic brackets are all honest recomputations against live dome data, not hardcoded assertions.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — the section fairly quotes the dome author's own caveats and credits them before making the acknowledge-without-fix critique. The Kernel of Truth section acknowledges genuine rigor improvements and frames the critique conditionally (if classified-claim count grows → taxonomy is real; if stalls → narrative layer).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None material. Two minor internal-attribution issues flagged as holes below.

fisher_chi2_at_p01.claim: χ²(2k=1040) for 520 p-values at p=0.1 gives χ² ≈ 2,393 ≈ 30σ

fisher_chi2_at_p005.claim: χ²(2k=1040) for 520 p-values at p=0.05 gives χ² ≈ 3,115 ≈ 45σ

nonoverlap_tally.claim: 14 STATIC + 7 BORROWED + 2 TAUTOLOGY + 2 THEORY-FRAMEWORK + 2 LOOSE + 1 DISCRIMINATING = 28
nonoverlap_tally.check: 14+7+2+2+2+1 = 28; row sets match table body (3,4,17-28 for STATIC; 2,6-11 for BORROWED; 1,5 for TAUTOLOGY; 15,16 for THEORY-FRAMEWORK; 12,13 for LOOSE; 14 for DISCRIMINATING)

sigma_growth_factor.result: PASS — '~5×' is accurate; the attribution (40% more domains × √19 for repeat inflation) is a plausible decomposition

repeats_per_domain.result: MINOR IMPRECISION — '~19' rounds up by 2.3%; acceptable at '~' granularity but technically the factor is √18.57 ≈ 4.31×, not √19 ≈ 4.36×. Either factor produces the same conclusion. Not worth patching.

holes_found.description: The paragraph immediately after the non-overlap tally asserts 'λ_g = 8,619 km is the primary shared constant linking the firmament-height, geomagnetic-decay, and equatorial-gravity rows' without row-level markers in the 28-row table itself showing which rows are λ_g-dependent. A defender can ask: which of the 14 STATIC rows are actually λ_g-dependent? Without per-row tagging, the claim is asserted from prior-section authority (Part 2b.4 shared-ruler critique). Readers coming to 4.1 cold cannot verify the attribution.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) add a final column to the audit table marking the dominant shared constant per row (λ_g, κ, v_a, r_avg, disc_radius, sun_altitude, WGS84, IERS, classical-physics), or (b) add a short sub-paragraph after the non-overlap tally enumerating the 14 rows that are λ_g-dependent by name. Option (b) is the cheaper fix. The underlying arithmetic is correct; the hole is presentation, not substance.

holes_found.description: In the Bottom Line paragraph, the parenthetical '(λ_A is excluded because its "prediction" internally substitutes the standard-model Hubble constant H₀ = 70 km/s/Mpc)' reads as a preemptive exclusion note — but λ_A is already classified BORROWED at row 10 of the audit table, so nothing is being 'excluded' from the DISCRIMINATING count. The parenthetical looks like legacy text from an earlier version when λ_A might have been a discriminating candidate. As written, it defends against a strawman — a reader might infer 'so there were 2 discriminators but you excluded one,' which is not the argument.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) delete the parenthetical entirely (the BORROWED classification at row 10 already accomplishes the exclusion cleanly), or (b) reword to acknowledge λ_A is classified BORROWED above and this sentence is a reminder of why, not a fresh exclusion. Option (a) is cleaner.

holes_found.description: The 12-knob denominator in the 2.33× ratio uses the TOTAL knob space from formula_runtime.json — but the 28 scored dashboard rows do not appear to exercise all 12 knobs. Specifically, FORM-V51-005 FSF Station Scaling (0.19550, 0.1640, 32.974 — three knobs) is not visible in any of the 28 rows as a predicted-value source (it is used for Southern-Hemisphere geography in prior WINs like WIN-024, but the WIN-024 rows in the table are STATIC references to the prior WIN, not direct FORM-V51-005 evaluations). If only ~9 of the 12 knobs are in active use in the scored dashboard, the effective label-inflation ratio is 28/9 ≈ 3.1×, not 2.33×. Our 2.33× is CHARITABLE TO THE DOME — it could be tightened. A defender would not exploit this; a detail-minded reader might notice the knob-use asymmetry and ask why we used the larger denominator.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a single footnote or clause acknowledging the 12-knob count is the full formula-pack budget and that the effective knobs-used-by-scored-domains figure is somewhat lower (~9-10), making the 2.33× a LOWER BOUND on label inflation. This strengthens the argument against the dome without opening a flank. Do not change the headline ratio — 2.33× is the correct number against the published formula pack.

holes_found.description: The claim '~28 × 19 ≈ 520 time-series observations' in the independence-violation paragraph is arithmetically slightly off: 28 × 19 = 532, and 520/28 = 18.57. The re-evaluation factor is closer to ~18.6 than to 19. This produces a deflation factor of √18.57 ≈ 4.31× rather than √19 ≈ 4.36× — both round to 4.4×. Immaterial to the argument but a pedantic defender could note the rounding.
holes_found.recommendation: No change needed. The '~' qualifier covers the imprecision and the downstream √19 ≈ 4.4× factor survives either way. If a future edit touches this block, change '~19' to '~18-19' and √19 to √18-19. Not a blocker.

holes_found.description: Row 22 (Mascon Gravity Anomaly) is classified STATIC with an inline self-acknowledgment: 'STATIC outlier — predicted is a range, observed is a point, 23% tolerance is atypical for this bucket.' This is honest self-disclosure, but a defender could argue row 22 is more naturally LOOSE (wide tolerance, directional check) given the 23% tolerance and range-vs-point comparison. The argument for STATIC rests on the source string '(WIN-076 static win)' — fine, but the hybrid nature weakens the clean non-overlap claim that categorizations don't interact.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider either (a) moving row 22 to LOOSE (which would update the non-overlap tally to 13 STATIC + 3 LOOSE) or (b) adding a very short sentence after the non-overlap tally explicitly noting row 22 is classified by source-of-value rather than tolerance-band and inviting the reader to re-classify if they prefer. Not urgent — the self-acknowledgment in the table row already gives the defender nowhere to hide.

holes_found.description: The footnote 'Bookkeeping footnote' flags the 67 CONFIRMED / 69 confirmed mismatch on live.html as 'cosmetic two-count bookkeeping inconsistency' and says we 'do not build further argument on it.' Later sections (Timestamp Error tab, EXP-183 Registry Framing Audit) may in fact make further argument on related bookkeeping mismatches (9/69 taxonomy coverage, 10/9/1 Live Power vs wins.html vs strict_prospective gaps). The Section 4.1 footnote's 'do not build further argument' framing is slightly out of sync with the broader review's treatment of bookkeeping gaps as a pattern.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider replacing 'we note it here for completeness but do not build further argument on it' with 'we note it here for completeness; see the Timestamp Error tab and Part 4.2 Registry Framing Audit for the broader acknowledge-without-fix pattern this instance belongs to.' Defer until EXP-183 lands. Not urgent.

holes_found.description: The 'divided audience' framing (humans see 47.69σ, AI auditors see caveats) is an effective rhetorical device, but the phrase 'This is the same acknowledge-without-fix pattern visible in the dome's OpenTimestamps narrative adoption (see the Timestamp Error tab)' depends on the reader knowing what that phrase means. Given Part 4 appears in the 'pages' tab, a reader jumping directly to Part 4.1 may not have the OTS canary context. Consider one inline clause defining the pattern ('acknowledgement in API-layer files or comments without structural change to the visible site').
holes_found.recommendation: Add a 6-8 word inline clause defining 'acknowledge-without-fix pattern' on first use in the section, then continue linking to the Timestamp Error tab for the full treatment. Not urgent.

stronger_arguments.description: The 'divided audience' critique can be sharpened by naming what contractual obligation the ai_manifest.json caveats impose and on whom. The dome author is essentially instructing third-party AI reviewers (Claude, Gemini, etc.) to not trust the headline tombstone numbers he himself displays. This is a de-facto admission that the headline numbers are not the scientific signal. If a dome defender says 'but the author is being transparent,' our reply is: transparency to a tiny audience (AI auditors reading API files) does not cure misrepresentation to the primary audience (humans viewing live.html). Consider adding a one-sentence framing: 'The author's own ai_manifest.json tells AI readers not to treat the tombstone numbers as validation — meaning the author does not ask third-party AI auditors to do what he asks human visitors to do, which is read those numbers as validation.'

The shared-ruler argument could be strengthened by showing the specific arithmetic that Fisher inputs from λ_g-dependent domains are not only non-independent but are literally functions of the same parameter evaluated at different r. If λ_g moves by 1%, all 14 λ_g-dependent p-values move together — the Fisher χ²-contribution from those 14 is not 14 independent draws but effectively 1 draw replicated 14 times. Under that framing, the Fisher degrees-of-freedom should be counted as ~1 (for λ_g) + individual-knob DOFs, giving perhaps 6-8 effective DOFs rather than 28×19 = 520. This is a much sharper inflation factor than the √19 time-series critique.
sources: standard treatment of correlated Fisher combinations (Brown 1975; Kost & McDermott 2002)

stronger_arguments.description: The Kernel of Truth 'falsifiable test' (does claim_index.json grow from 9 toward 69?) would be stronger if the test included a time-bound threshold. Without a specific cadence, the test can fail silently — a defender could always say 'in progress.' Suggest: 'If claim_index.json has fewer than ~25 classified claims by 2026-Q4 (a 16-claim-per-quarter expansion rate), the rigor improvement is plausibly narrative-layer. If it has more than 40 by end of 2026, the taxonomy is doing real audit work.' A concrete number forces the falsifiability.

kernel_of_truth.description: The section itself already includes a full Kernel-of-truth sub-section crediting the dome author for (a) the five-class taxonomy definition, (b) the correct OpenTimestamps scoping in methodology.json, (c) the registry_semantics + agent_rules instructions to AI auditors, and (d) the open publication of formula_runtime.json. This is the right level of credit — genuine rigor improvements in definition, but not yet broadly applied. The kernel is not understated.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Definitional rigor without broad application is a tool, not a result. The taxonomy currently classifies 9 of 69+ claims; it cannot retire the tombstone numbers until it covers them. The AI-auditor caveats in ai_manifest.json contradict the human-facing tombstones on the same site — that is itself the problem, not the resolution. The formula-pack publication is what lets us count 12 knobs / 28 labels and expose the 2.33× label inflation. The kernel, properly credited, reinforces rather than undercuts the critique.

deeper_analysis: This is a strong post-integration section. The central arithmetic (Fisher at p=0.1 → 30σ, at p=0.05 → 45σ bracketing the dome's 47.69σ) is correct, the 12-knob recount against formula_runtime.json is defensible, and the non-overlap 28-row tally sums correctly. The TLDR is accurate. The divided-audience framing is rhetorically effective and the 'acknowledge-without-fix pattern' naming ties 4.1 to the Timestamp Error canary cleanly. The three moderate holes are all presentation-level: (1) missing per-row shared-constant markers to back the 'λ_g is the primary ruler' claim, (2) a redundant λ_A exclusion parenthetical in Bottom Line, (3) the 12-knob denominator is charitable (effective knobs-used ≈ 9-10, so 2.33× is a lower bound). None undermine the thesis. The minor holes are rounding imprecision (~19 vs ~18.6) and a single classification tension on row 22 (STATIC vs LOOSE), both self-flagged by the section itself. Overall: current_verdict_holds = true with minor presentation edits recommended. The EXP-182 fold-in correctly supersedes EXP-178 (WIN-073/075/076 dome-namespace disambiguation now flagged in Notation paragraph and table rows 15, 20, 22), EXP-179 (12-knob / 2.33× recount folded into main body), and EXP-180 (NMP Longitude now DISCRIMINATING, BORROWED count updated to 7). The 2b.5 stub pointing to 4.1 preserves the capstone anchor in Part 2b while moving the 15k-char audit to its proper home — this is the correct architectural move.

citations_unchecked: GitHub commit a57e731 for Nhughes09/predictions — specific commit hash cited for the daily-review AI pipeline; not verified against GitHub in this review. The analyst noted live verification at draft time. If the commit does not exist or describes something different, it is a citation bug.

Specific quoted phrases from ai_manifest.json and methodology.json (e.g., 'bookkeeping for the current registry state', 'separate discriminating claims from supportive and pending items') — not verified by fetching the live JSON in this review. The analyst verified against live fetches at draft time per EXP-182 revision_notes.
